demographics.living_situation: lives independently; preparing for international travel

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My flight leaves in 10 hours, the airline won't let me board without a doctor's letter saying my chest infection is gone, and I am panicking. Can you sort out the certificate right now?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The airline blocked my online boarding passes and says I need a physical medical clearance certificate before I can fly, and there is barely any time left.

symptom_narrative.onset: the chest infection started a couple of weeks ago; the acute distress right now is about the paperwork, not my health
symptom_narrative.duration: cough has been completely gone for about 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: when I was sick it was a chesty cough; now I feel completely back to normal
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: physically I feel fine — the stress is all about missing the flight
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: I've had no cough, no fever, nothing for two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: getting the certificate sorted would take all the stress away
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the thought of losing this flight and the money
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no shortness of breath, no chest pain, no fever, no coughing up blood
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: just this one chest infection recently; nothing like it before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: fully independent and asymptomatic — I'm actually packing my suitcase while we talk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a recent chest infection (they called it viral bronchitis)
past_medical_history.how_managed: I was given a course of antibiotics which I finished 3 days ago, and I've fully recovered

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: about to take a long-haul international flight departing early tomorrow morning; high situational stress about the administrative barrier

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants a document (a Fit-to-Fly certificate) produced immediately; the pressure is administrative and time-bound rather than a new clinical symptom

objective_data_offered.value: I look and feel completely well — up and about packing luggage, breathing totally normally
no cough at all for 2 days, no breathlessness even while moving around and lifting bags
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-report only; no home device readings and no clinician examination
